findings.title: Tests fail on dirty DB — not idempotent without prior cleanup
findings.detail: First run produced 8 failures due to stale ledger rows from a prior interrupted developer test session. The beforeAll clears the two test brand IDs but cannot clean state seeded by other callers. Second and third runs were 16/16 green. The afterEach hook clears properly within a session. Not a VETO finding: the gate is structurally sound and CI starts with a clean DB; local dev can hit this if tests are interrupted. Recommend a dedicated test schema isolation or truncation guard.

findings.title: ISO-2 cross-brand isolation test is structurally weak — proves absence-of-data, not RLS block
findings.detail: The ISO-2 test seeds Brand B rows and checks that Brand A's engine returns 0. It passes because Brand A has no seeded rows, not because RLS blocked Brand A from seeing Brand B's rows. To truly prove RLS isolation, the test should seed Brand A rows, then run the engine with Brand B's GUC and expect 0. The current structure cannot distinguish 'RLS blocked' from 'no rows'. ISO-1 (current_user=brain_app, non-superuser) and ISO-3 (empty GUC → fail-closed) are sound. This is a test-coverage gap on the isolation proof, not an engine bug.

tests_run.name: test:parity:@brain/tool-parity-oracle (run 1)
tests_run.evidence: 8/16 failed — stale ledger rows from prior dev session; afterEach/beforeAll cleared on 2nd run

tests_run.name: test:parity:@brain/tool-parity-oracle (run 2)
tests_run.evidence: 16/16 passed; F1-F5 all green; RED PROOF captured; ISO-1/2/3 passed

tests_run.name: RED_PROOF_live_perturbation
tests_run.evidence: Perturbed engine +1n: 8 tests RED (delta=1 for all INR fixtures). Reverted: 16/16 GREEN. Gate is non-tautological.

tests_run.name: turbo:test:parity:--affected:--dry-run
tests_run.evidence: @brain/metric-engine and @brain/tool-parity-oracle both in scope; tool depends on @brain/metric-engine#build

tests_run.name: ci_gate_no_continue_on_error
tests_run.evidence: pr.yml:33 has no continue-on-error; parity step is blocking

tests_run.name: validity_check (no --require-negative-control)
tests_run.evidence: Exit 0, 9 files scanned, no bypass-green or tautology patterns found

tests_run.name: validity_check (--require-negative-control)
tests_run.evidence: Exit 3 — flagged missing negative control. Remediated by QA engineer: live perturbation RED proof executed (perturb +1n → 8 RED → revert → 16 GREEN)

tests_run.name: no_float_check
tests_run.evidence: No parseFloat/Math.abs in metric-engine or oracle money paths; all money fields bigint

tests_run.name: oracle_non_tautological_import_check
tests_run.evidence: reference.ts imports only 'pg'; zero @brain/metric-engine imports; zero calls to realized_gmv_as_of or provisional_gmv_as_of

negative_control.captured_red_output: 8/16 tests FAILED: F1 delta=1 (engine=50001n, ref=50000n); F2 delta=1 (engine=1n, ref=0n); F3 delta=1 (engine=35001n, ref=35000n); F4/F5 all delta=1 failures; ISO-2 delta=1; PER-CURRENCY delta=1; NO-BLEND delta=1
